name: Mushroom & Butternut Squash Lasagne
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces lasagna noodles (dry or 1 lb fresh)
1 1/2 pounds butternut squash, peeled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook noodles in a large pot of boiling water until barely tender (8 minutes for dried, 1 minute for fresh). Drain and rinse under cold water. Spread noodles on clean kitchen towels, cover with plastic wrap, and set aside.
2. In a small bowl, combine sun-dried tomatoes and dried porcini mushrooms. Add 1 cup boiling water, cover, and let stand for 10 minutes. Lift out the tomatoes and mushrooms, chop them, strain the soaking liquid through a fine sieve, and set aside.
3. Heat 1 cup of milk in a saucepan over medium heat until steaming. In a small bowl, whisk together remaining flour with the hot milk until smooth. Add this mixture to the saucepan and stir constantly over heat until thickened and simmering. Continue cooking for 1 minute. Remove from heat, whisk in cream cheese, add 2/3 cup of spaghetti sauce and vinegar, season with salt and pepper.
4. Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add onions, carrots, and garlic, sauté until soft (about 2 minutes). Add fresh mushrooms, rosemary, reserved tomatoes, and porcini; cook until mushrooms are just wilted (about 2 minutes longer). Stir in remaining flour. Add reserved soaking liquid and the remaining spaghetti sauce. Cook until thickened (about 1 minute). Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F. Lightly spray a 9 x 13 inch baking sheet with cooking spray or oil.
6. Spread half of the sauce on bottom of prepared dish, line with a layer of noodles. Add half of the mushroom mixture and sprinkle with Parmesan. Top with another layer of noodles, squash (seasoned with salt and pepper), and half of the remaining sauce. Add another layer of noodles, remaining mushroom mixture, and Parmesan. Finish with remaining noodles and sauce.
7. Cover baking dish tightly with aluminum foil or cooking spray-coated foil. Bake for 30 minutes. Remove foil and bake for an additional 10 to 15 minutes, or until lightly browned and bubbling. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.
According to Eating Well: 325 calories, 13 grams protein, 6 grams fat (2.4 grams saturated), 58 grams carbohydrates, 275 mg sodium, 9 mg cholesterol, 5 grams fiber.
According to Master Cook: 512 calories, 8.5 grams fat.
